acceptedQuestions.subject: Economy
acceptedQuestions.prompt: In the hierarchy of economic integration, which of the following features primarily distinguishes a 'Common Market' from a 'Customs Union'?
contextLines: Basics of Free Trade Agreements and Trade Hierarchy

options.text: The elimination of all internal tariffs and quotas between member nations
The adoption of a common external tariff policy against non-member countries
The free movement of factors of production, such as labour and capital
The establishment of common economic policies and shared institutions

acceptedQuestions.explanation: While both a Customs Union and a Common Market involve zero internal duties and a common external tariff (Options A and B), the defining distinction is that a Common Market also allows for the free movement of labour and capital (factors of production) and harmonized technical standards. Option D refers to an Economic Union, which is the deepest level of integration.
acceptedQuestions.takeaway: The free movement of labour and capital is the key factor that elevates a Customs Union to a Common Market.
acceptedQuestions.evidenceExcerpt: Custom Union involves zero duty trade among member countries and common tariffs against the rest of the world. Common Market allows free movement of labour and capital, along with harmonized technical standards.
acceptedQuestions.reviewNotes: High-quality distractor-based question that requires precise knowledge of the hierarchy of trade integration.

acceptedQuestions.subject: Economy
acceptedQuestions.prompt: In the context of international trade, Free Trade Agreements (FTAs) are considered a sanctioned exception to which of the following principles of the World Trade Organisation (WTO)?
contextLines: Basics of Free Trade Agreements and Trade Hierarchy
acceptedQuestions.explanation: The Most Favoured Nation (MFN) principle, a cornerstone of the WTO, requires that any trade advantage granted to one member must be extended to all other members. However, FTAs allow member countries to grant preferential treatment (such as lower tariffs) to their specific trade partners within the agreement without extending those same benefits to all other WTO members, thus acting as a legal exception to the MFN rule.
acceptedQuestions.takeaway: FTAs are a sanctioned exception to the WTO's MFN principle, allowing for preferential trade liberalization between specific partners.
acceptedQuestions.evidenceExcerpt: FTAs act as an exception to the Most Favoured Nation (MFN) principle of the World Trade Organisation (WTO).
acceptedQuestions.reviewNotes: Standard UPSC conceptual question testing the relationship between regional trade agreements and global trade rules.

acceptedQuestions.subject: Economy
acceptedQuestions.prompt: With reference to international trade negotiations, the term 'Early Harvest Scheme' is best described as:
contextLines: Basics of Free Trade Agreements and Trade Hierarchy

options.text: A mechanism to provide immediate subsidies to the agricultural sector before a trade deal is signed
A precursor to a Free Trade Agreement where specific products are identified for early tariff liberalization
A temporary suspension of trade barriers during a global economic crisis to boost demand
The final stage of an Economic Union where all technical standards are harmonized

acceptedQuestions.explanation: An Early Harvest Scheme (EHS) is a tactical precursor to a full FTA or other comprehensive trade agreements. It allows the negotiating parties to identify certain products for immediate tariff reduction or elimination to build confidence and momentum before concluding a more comprehensive agreement.
acceptedQuestions.takeaway: Early Harvest Schemes serve as confidence-building precursors to more comprehensive trade agreements.
acceptedQuestions.evidenceExcerpt: Early Harvest Scheme is a precursor to an FTA and other trade agreements.
acceptedQuestions.reviewNotes: Tests a specific term frequently used in Indian trade diplomacy, grounded in the provided text.

reviewerNotes: The selected questions cover the three distinct conceptual pillars of the provided text: the legal relationship between FTAs and the WTO (MFN exception), the procedural stages of trade negotiations (Early Harvest Scheme), and the structural hierarchy of economic integration (Customs Union vs. Common Market). Distractors were designed to be plausible within the domain of international trade.
